title: pelargonium × aurareum
intro: Pelargonium × aurantiorum, commonly known as the Cape Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the family Geraniaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in South African folk medicine, due to its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The extracts of this plant have been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. Pelargonium × aurantiorum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
